topic: Why can only 23 people push a shared birthday past fifty percent?

assets.question_hook: How can just 23 people already make a birthday match more likely than not?
assets.section_claim: The surprise starts before any formula because the intuition frame is wrong.
assets.visual_metaphor: twenty-three identity markers scattered around one hidden probability marker on a dark evidence table
assets.closing_takeaway: The paradox comes from relationship growth, not from remembering 365 separate days.

assets.question_hook: How can just 23 people already make a birthday match more likely than not?
assets.section_claim: Counting calendar days feels natural but misses where the growth really happens.
assets.visual_metaphor: a half-circle stage of person markers with a few muted red paths showing the wrong counting instinct
assets.closing_takeaway: The paradox comes from relationship growth, not from remembering 365 separate days.

assets.question_hook: How can just 23 people already make a birthday match more likely than not?
assets.section_claim: Each added person connects to everyone already present.
assets.visual_metaphor: one highlighted marker in front of a grid of earlier markers with connection lines radiating outward
assets.relationship_object: numbered markers in a structured crowd
assets.comparison_unit: relationship growth across the whole group
assets.closing_takeaway: The paradox comes from relationship growth, not from remembering 365 separate days.

assets.question_hook: How can just 23 people already make a birthday match more likely than not?
assets.section_claim: The no-match space shrinks quickly once comparisons pile up.
assets.visual_metaphor: compressed probability curves and dense comparison traces converging into one dominant result marker
assets.closing_takeaway: The paradox comes from relationship growth, not from remembering 365 separate days.

assets.question_hook: How can just 23 people already make a birthday match more likely than not?
assets.section_claim: The right counting unit is the relationship set, not the single date.
assets.visual_metaphor: two note cards facing each other, one for intuition and one for grouped relationships, on an aged desk with subtle correction marks
assets.relationship_object: paired note cards and marginal annotations
assets.closing_takeaway: Many paradoxes disappear once you count relationships instead of isolated points.

remotion_update_rule: After image generation, write the generated relative path into scene.visual.asset or scene.visual.generatedAssetPath, keep scene.visual.assetMode as generated, and retain the bundled validation panel as fallback only.
